concerns.text: FR-007 defines a 'Null Model Baseline' by shuffling feature values to distinguish drift from noise. However, no User Story (US-1, US-2, or US-3) explicitly requests or describes a null model generation step. US-2 focuses on 'quantifying drift' and US-3 on 'significance testing', but neither mentions the specific mechanism of shuffling features to create a baseline distribution. This FR appears to be an unanchored scope creep (orphaned requirement) as it introduces a complex statistical control not requested in the user goals.

SC-004 states 'The validity of the drift signal is measured against the Null Model Baseline (FR-007)'. Since FR-007 is not anchored to any User Story (see concern above), SC-004 is also orphaned. There is no user story that establishes the goal of validating drift against a null model of shuffled features. The success criteria must trace back to a stated user goal.

User Story 3 describes applying 'a permutation-based significance test' to the correlation sequence. However, the specific implementation of this test (shuffling the time order of windows vs. shuffling feature values) is ambiguous in the story. FR-008 anchors to US-3, but FR-007 (shuffling features) is also linked to US-2 in the text but not explicitly in the story description. The story needs to explicitly clarify if the 'permutation test' involves shuffling time windows (to test temporal stability) or features (to test feature relevance), as the current text conflates these distinct statistical goals.
